engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Duster Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Duster in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Duster K9K engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector wear - high-pressure piezo injectors degrade, causing rough idle and white smoke.
Turbo failure - variable geometry turbo actuator fails from carbon buildup at high mileage.

cards.altText: Renault Duster H5F engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - premature chain wear causes rattling and catastrophic engine failure.
Excessive oil consumption - piston ring design flaw leads to severe oil burning and catalytic converter damage.

cards.altText: Renault Duster H4M engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Coil pack failure - ignition coils degrade, causing misfires and engine management lights.
Throttle body carbon buildup - causes hesitation and rough idling on city-driven examples.

commonProblems.h2: Renault Duster Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Duster relies heavily on the 1.5 dCi diesel and the controversial 1.2 TCe petrol engines. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: 1.2 TCe Timing Chain & Oil Consumption - The "Motorgate" Crisis
problems.rootCause: Premature timing chain stretch and a flawed piston ring design that causes severe oil consumption, eventually leading to catalytic converter failure and engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing chain, tensioners, and guides replaced before catastrophic failure.
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life by 50k+ miles if caught before oil loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine stripped, block replaced with updated pistons, cylinder head retained.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full reliability with corrected factory parts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt H5F unit with upgraded timing chain and oil control rings.
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; includes 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.recommendation: If your Renault Duster 1.2 TCe is rattling on cold starts or consuming more than a litre of oil every 1,000 miles, do not delay. A preventative timing chain update is viable, but if oil consumption has already damaged the catalytic converter, a fully rebuilt engine with updated pistons is the only cost-effective fix.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt H5F engine prices for your Renault Duster

problems.h4: 1.5 dCi Injector & Turbo Failure - High Mileage Diesel Issues
problems.rootCause: Piezo injector wear causes uneven fuel delivery and white smoke, while the variable geometry turbo actulator seizes from carbon buildup on short journeys.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Faulty injectors removed, tested, and replaced with refurbished units.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth idle; resolves white smoke within 24 months.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbocharger and actuator replaced, oil feed pipes renewed.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full boost pressure; requires fresh oil and filter.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt K9K engine with new injectors and turbo included.
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if turbo failure caused sump wash and bearing damage.

problems.recommendation: The 1.5 dCi is a robust workhorse, but injectors and turbos are consumables at high mileage. We recommend replacing individual components via a specialist rather than a full engine replacement, unless there is evidence of metal debris in the sump or severe cylinder scoring.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Renault Duster 1.5 dCi turbo and injector repairs

problems.h4: 1.5 dCi DPF & EGR Blockage - The Urban Driving Penalty
problems.rootCause: Frequent short trips prevent passive DPF regeneration, leading to severe soot buildup that contaminates the EGR valve and restricts exhaust flow.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical DPF clean and software-forced active regeneration cycle.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if DPF is not physically cracked or melted.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished DPF and EGR valve fitted, with software reset.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores emissions compliance; requires regular motorway driving.

problems.recommendation: If your Renault Duster is in limp mode with a flashing glow plug light, start with a professional chemical clean. However, if the DPF ash mass exceeds 100g, replacement is unavoidable. Ensure your specialist uses a quality unit with a 12-month warranty.
problems.cta: Find DPF and EGR cleaning services for your Renault Duster

items.title: Renault Duster 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L dCi diesel is the backbone of the Renault Duster range, dominating UK sales with its strong torque and excellent fuel economy. Available in multiple tunes, it is the most common engine requiring replacement due to high-mileage wear on fuel and turbo systems.

items.title: Renault Duster 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L TCe turbo petrol was introduced to offer stronger performance than the naturally aspirated units, but it is heavily associated with the "Motorgate" timing chain and oil consumption scandals that drive significant replacement demand.
commonFailurePoints: excessive oil consumption due to piston ring design
items.cta: Compare Renault Duster 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Duster 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L 16V is a robust, naturally aspirated petrol engine sourced from Nissan and adapted by Renault. It lacks the turbo complexity of the TCe units, making it a reliable choice for budget-conscious buyers, though it struggles with fuel economy.
commonFailurePoints: minor oil leaks from valve cover gaskets.
items.cta: Compare Renault Duster 1.6L petrol engine prices

families: H5F - The 1.2 TCe turbo, notorious for timing chain and oil consumption failures.

Newer 1.3 TCe units have resolved the timing chain issues of the older 1.2L.

engineYears.h2: Renault Duster Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Duster's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The launch of the Duster in the UK introduced the rugged 1.5 dCi diesel and the controversial 1.2 TCe petrol to the budget SUV market.
keyChanges: Renault Duster launched in the UK as a budget-friendly 4x4 capable SUV.
Introduction of the K9K 1.5 dCi diesel and H5F 1.2 TCe petrol engines.
H4M 1.6L naturally aspirated petrol added for entry-level trims.
knownFor: Unbeatable value for money and genuine off-road capability.
The 1.2 TCe engines from this era are highly prone to timing chain and oil consumption failures.
Interior plastics are cheap but incredibly durable and easy to clean.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2016 Renault Duster replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Duster 1.2 TCe engine failure • Renault Duster 1.5 dCi turbo replacement • Renault Duster 1.6 engine misfire

years.description: A major cosmetic update brought improved interiors and refined versions of the existing K9K and H5F engines, though the 1.2 TCe flaws persisted.
keyChanges: Comprehensive facelift with updated interior quality and infotainment.
Introduction of the K9K 830/832 Blue dCi engines with improved emissions.
1.2 TCe 125 retained, continuing to suffer from Motorgate-related failures.
knownFor: Improved sound deadening and cabin quality over the Mk1.
DPF issues became more prominent on the 1.5 dCi as emissions systems grew more complex.
The 1.2 TCe remained a high-risk engine for catastrophic failure.
years.cta: Compare prices for 2017-2020 Renault Duster engine replacement

years.description: The all-new Mk2 Duster adopted the modern CMG-B platform and finally retired the flawed 1.2 TCe in favor of the reliable 1.3 TCe and new 1.0 TCe units.
keyChanges: Complete redesign on the modern CMG-B platform with improved safety and tech.
The problematic 1.2 TCe is replaced by the robust 1.3 TCe and new 1.0 TCe petrols.
1.5 Blue dCi diesel remains, updated to meet strict Euro 6d emissions standards.
knownFor: Vastly improved on-road manners and safety ratings over the Mk1.
The 1.3 TCe is widely praised for resolving the timing chain nightmares of the 1.2L.
Early examples are just now reaching the age where replacement enquiries might begin.
years.cta: Enquire about 2021+ Renault Duster engine availability

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Renault Duster was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Renault Duster specialists.

faq.h2: Renault Duster Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Renault Duster engine?
items.answer: A Renault Duster engine replacement typically costs between £850 and £2,400 for the engine itself, depending on the engine code and condition. Supply and fit services will add approximately £500 to £900 in labour charges.
keyPoints: 1.5 dCi diesel engines usually range from £850 to £1,600.
1.2 TCe petrol replacements cost more, between £1,100 and £2,200.
Labour for supply and fit adds £500 to £900 depending on the garage.
All engines from EngineMarket include a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
items.cta: Compare Renault Duster engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Renault Duster 1.2 TCe engine unreliable?
items.answer: Yes, the 1.2 TCe (H5F) engine fitted to the Renault Duster between 2012 and 2018 is highly unreliable due to the "Motorgate" scandal involving premature timing chain stretch and severe oil consumption.
keyPoints: Timing chains stretch prematurely, causing cold-start rattling and potential failure.
Flawed piston rings lead to excessive oil burning and catalytic converter damage.
If your Duster has an H5F engine, budget for a replacement or upgraded rebuild.
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt H5F Renault Duster engines with upgraded internals

items.question: What is the most reliable engine in the Renault Duster?
items.answer: The 1.6 16V naturally aspirated petrol (H4M) and the newer 1.3 TCe (H5H) are the most reliable engines in the Renault Duster range, avoiding the turbo and timing chain issues of the 1.2 TCe.
keyPoints: The 1.6L H4M is a simple, Nissan-sourced unit with minimal complex components.
The 1.3L H5H was specifically designed to fix the flaws of the older 1.2 TCe.
The 1.5 dCi diesel is robust but requires strict maintenance of the turbo and injectors.
items.cta: Find reliable Renault Duster engine options with a 12-month warranty

items.question: How long do Renault Duster 1.5 dCi engines last?
items.answer: The 1.5 dCi (K9K) diesel engine in the Renault Duster can easily exceed 150,000 miles, provided the turbo, injectors, and timing belt are maintained according to strict service intervals.
keyPoints: Timing belt must be replaced every 4 years or 80,000 miles to prevent snap.
Injectors and turbo actuators commonly need attention between 80k and 120k miles.
DPF blockage is a major risk if the vehicle is only used for short city trips.
items.cta: Compare 1.5 dCi Renault Duster engine prices and specialist rebuilds

items.question: Can I fit a used engine to my Renault Duster?
items.answer: Yes, you can fit a used engine to your Renault Duster, but it carries higher risk than a rebuilt unit. Ensure any used engine is compression tested and comes with at least a 3-month warranty.
keyPoints: Used engines are the cheapest option, typically costing £600 to £1,200.
Always verify the mileage and service history of the donor vehicle.
Rebuilt engines are safer as they replace all internal wear components.
items.cta: Check used Renault Duster engine availability from vetted UK breakers

items.question: What causes the engine management light on a Renault Duster?
items.answer: The engine management light on a Renault Duster is most commonly triggered by DPF blockage, EGR valve failure, or failing fuel injectors on diesel models, and ignition coil faults on petrol models.
keyPoints: Diesel models often trigger the light due to soot buildup in the EGR or DPF.
Petrol models frequently suffer from coil pack failures causing misfires.
A diagnostic scan is required to read the specific fault code before replacing parts.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Duster engine diagnostics and repair specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Renault Duster?
items.answer: Yes, replacing the engine on a Renault Duster is usually worth it, as the vehicle's low purchase price means a £1,500 rebuilt engine represents a small fraction of the cost of buying a replacement vehicle.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine restores the car for a fraction of the price of a new Duster.
Supply and fit services make the process completely hassle-free.
A 12-month warranty ensures you are protected against future failures.
items.cta: Compare Renault Duster engine replacement costs vs buying a new car

items.question: Where is the engine code located on a Renault Duster?
items.answer: The engine code on a Renault Duster is located on a sticker in the service book, on the driver's door B-pillar sticker, or stamped directly onto the engine block near the gearbox bell housing.
keyPoints: Check your V5C logbook or the sticker in the driver's door shut for the code.
Common codes include K9K for diesel, and H5F, H4M for petrol.
Entering your registration into EngineMarket instantly identifies your exact code.
items.cta: Enter your reg to find your Renault Duster engine code and replacement prices

items.question: Does a Renault Duster replacement engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every replacement engine supplied through the EngineMarket network comes with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, covering all internal components and giving you complete peace of mind.
keyPoints: Rebuilt and reconditioned units include a 12-month warranty as standard.
Used engines typically carry a shorter 30 to 90-day warranty.
Always confirm the exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before paying.
items.cta: Find Renault Duster engines with a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty

items.question: Should I choose a diesel or petrol Renault Duster?
items.answer: Choose a diesel Renault Duster if you do high mileage or frequent off-road driving, but opt for the 1.3 TCe petrol if you mostly drive in urban areas to avoid DPF blockage issues.
keyPoints: The 1.5 dCi diesel offers superior torque and fuel economy for motorway and off-road use.
Urban driving will block the diesel DPF; petrol is better for short city trips.
Avoid the 1.2 TCe petrol; only consider the 1.6L or the newer 1.3 TCe.
